login.new_wallet_help1: Your wallet will be stored in your browser. Wallet password is required to open wallet and see the accounts within the wallet and for signing transactions.
login.new_wallet_help2: We recommend the password to be 12 or more characters long, using lower case letters, uppercase letters, numbers and special characters.

swap.title: Swap assets
swap.has_sk: Only standard accounts with private keys are supported now

swap.swap_asset_to: Select asset to which you want to swap
swap.apps_optin: SWAP requires your account to opt in to the applications

gateway.title: Free open source payment gateway without a middleman
gateway.perex: Start accepting algo, USDC, USDT or other assets on your website. By accepting cryptocurrency without a financial middleman you will increase your sales on your website and increase your profit.
gateway.create_order: Create a unique matching symbol, e.g. order id
gateway.customer_pays: Customer pays directly to your algorand account
gateway.customer_redirected: Customer will be redirected back to your website after successful payment
gateway.use_your_money: Use your money. You can exchange USDT and take USD from the exchange, or you can pay with USDT for other goods or services
gateway.you_make_details: You make the payment details. You setup the successful and cancel URL address. We notify you when the payment is done. You have to check in your backend where you accept the payment that it has been received and you notify the customer.
gateway.opensource: This project is open source. You can clone, modify, or run it in your own private environment for commercial purposes
gateway.just_website: The payment gateway is just an HTML website with javascript. No other backend is used other than official algorand nodes.
gateway.no_restrictions: You do not need an {name} account in order to use the gateway.
gateway.parameters_perex: You can set any of these parameters. The only required fields include the address where you want to receive your payment and the transaction amount. The payment address consists of two parts: payment info, and gateway settings. Both fields are base64 encoded separated by "/". Please make sure that you url-encode them properly. Payment info is the basic algorand protocol address used for example in the QR codes.
gateway.amount: use the asset base unit. E.g. 1 ALGO = 1000000
gateway.fee: the amount in microalgos. E.g. 0.001 ALGO = 1000
gateway.success: URL address to which the client is redirected after successful payment
gateway.cancel: URL address to which the client is redirected after he clicks cancel payment
gateway.asset: You can specify asset id for the transaction
gateway.error_transaction: Please fill in at least account, amount and asset
gateway.error_url: URL address must start with https://

receive.title: Receive payment
receive.decimals: Information about decimal place to QR code
receive.label: Suggested label of the receiver address

acc_overview.title: Account overview
acc_overview.name: Name
acc_overview.delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this account from your wallet?
acc_overview.not_funded: Account {addr} at the network {network} is not funded yet.
acc_overview.generate_next_hd_help: Derive another account from the same HD wallet mnemonic at the next iteration.
acc_overview.no_assets: There are no assets yet on this account
acc_overview.pay_help: Send money or tokens to other accounts in the network.
acc_overview.swap_help: You can swap one token to another using DEX aggregator services
acc_overview.rekey_help: If you are afraid that your account private key may be compromise, you can rekey your account to new private key and keep your original address safe.
acc_overview.asset_optin_help: If you want to receive asset on algorand network you have to open the account for that token.
acc_overview.receive_payment_help: You can ask your customers or friends to send you money. This page will generate payment QR code so that others can do it on one click.
acc_overview.export_help: You can generate shamir backup mnemonics or algorand mnemonic for accounts with private keys.
acc_overview.arc14_help: ARC 14 is authentication standard utilizing signed transactions. You can generate the authentication tokens on this page.
acc_overview.asset_optinArc200_help: You can watch on your account the ARC200 assets. ARC200 assets are smart contracts with feature that anyone can opt in user's account to be able to receive the asset. ARC200 assets may be dangerous and you should require trustworthy audit of the smart contract so that you can fully trust it.
acc_overview.scheduled_payment_help: You can setup and manage a scheduled periodic payments to another accounts
acc_overview.arc14msig_process: Please sign ARC14 authentication transaction and return back to this form.
acc_overview.generating_keys: Generating participation keys.. Please be patient, the participation node is performing CPU sensitive task.
acc_overview.making_account_online: Setting your account to online state. Please wait a while
acc_overview.making_account_offline: Setting your account to offline state. Please wait a while

optin.optin_confirm: Are you sure you want to pay 0.001 algo to opt in to this asset? After you opt in, you will be able to receive this asset in your account.
optin.asset_opt_in_success: Successfully opted in for the asset

newacc.title: New account
newacc.name: Internal account name
newacc.multisig_help: Multisignature account can process transactions only if N accounts listed at the account creation sign the transaction.
newacc.select_account_from_list: Select existing accounts in your wallet
newacc.add_other_accounts: Add your friends' accounts - one account per line
newacc.trashold_help: Select how many accounts are required to sign the transaction
newacc.create_account_help: In order to create the account, make sure to backup mnemonic phrase. Is it safe to show the phrase now?
newacc.position_question: What is the word at position n.
newacc.mnemonic_help: Write down the mnemonic words in the displayed order, copy or take a picture. Next step will be to confirm some of the words from this phrase and we will save it to the encrypted storage in your browser. It is safe to store this mnemonic and use this address without storing it to the account. Make sure never to lose the mnemonic phrase.
newacc.auto_lock_off: Auto lock feature is turned off
newacc.create_basic_help: Algorand basic account is ED25519 account with private key. This method uses your computer random number generator to create secret for your account.
newacc.ledger_account_help: One of most secure solutions is to handle the secret key generation by specialized device. This account utilizes Ledger device to create your account and handle the signing operations.
newacc.wc_account_help: You can connect to your friend's wallet using the wallet connect account type. You can send them request to sign the message for example as part of multisig.
newacc.twofa_account_help: Two factor account is multisig account, where one account is account of your choosing for example ledger account or email password account, second account is recovery account - ideally account which is not in the same secure storage, and third account is the two factor account from the server which signs transactions when you provide correct two factor pin code.
newacc.email_pass_account_help: ARC76 Email & Password account allows you to generate algorand account from any text. The text is hashed and generates 32 bytes as seed for your algorand account. This may be alternative to other types of random number generators in your computer or hw device which you can utilize in two factor account or generic multisig account.
newacc.create_vanity_help: You can brute force nice account address for your ED25519 account. This method simply tries a lot of combinations of creating account and lets you pick first or last letters of the account address.
newacc.create_multisign_account_help: Multisig accounts are the most secure accounts. If used in combination of multiple ledger devices and different random number generators, this provides enterprise level of security which is hardcoded directly to the algorand blockchain.
newacc.watch_account_help: You can track any account on the network.
newacc.import_account_help: You can recover the account from the algorand recovery mnemonic.
newacc.shamir_account_help: You can recover the account from the shamir backup recovery mnemonics.
newacc.hd_wallet_account_help: Create or import an ARC-52 HD wallet. A single mnemonic can derive many independent accounts, letting you generate as many accounts as you need from one backup.
newacc.no_accounts_at_network: There are no accounts for selected network

arc76account.title: Email and password account (ARC-0076)
arc76account.description: Hash of combination of your email and your password will generate seed bytes for your first algorand account. You can change the password or email only by rekeying to another account.
arc76account.description2: ARC-0076 standard will be used with algorithm PBKDF2-999999-SHA256. We recommend the password length to be at least 50 characters long. This type of account may be served as secondary type of random number generator and may strengthen your security. Please keep the secret secure and do not reuse it.
arc76account.arc_draft: ARC-0076 is in the DRAFT state. The specification can change. Please consider this implementation for experimental usage.
arc76account.select_password: Please select very very very secure password.
arc76account.password_not_stored: Watch out. We do not store your password. We will store only the account public address and email to the secure storage. When you will be doing transactions with this account you will be prompted to enter the password.
arc76account.gui_not_implemented: GUI for signing transaction without stored password is not currently implemented.

hdaccount.title: HD Wallet account (ARC-52)
hdaccount.description: HD (hierarchical deterministic) wallets use a single 24-word BIP-39 mnemonic to derive an unlimited number of independent Algorand accounts, following the ARC-52 (BIP32-Ed25519) standard. Create a new mnemonic or import an existing one to add its first account.
hdaccount.backup_warning: This mnemonic backs up every account derived from this HD wallet, not just this one. Keep it safe and never share it.
hdaccount.confirm_backup: I have safely backed up my mnemonic
hdaccount.write_mnemonic: Write down your 24 word BIP-39 mnemonic phrase
hdaccount.account_index_help: Optional. The hardened account index (m'/44'/283'/index'/0/0) used to derive this wallet's first account from the mnemonic. Leave at 0 unless you specifically need to recover a non-default iteration.
hdaccount.generate_next_help: Pick the iteration (account index) to derive the next account from this HD wallet's mnemonic. Each iteration produces a distinct, independent account address.
hdaccount.export_child_help: This account does not store its own secret. It is derived from the master mnemonic of its HD wallet root account. Back up the mnemonic from the root account instead.
hdaccount.not_backed_up_title: This account is not backed up!
hdaccount.not_backed_up_body: This HD wallet account was created automatically and its recovery mnemonic has not been backed up yet. If you lose access to this device without a backup, you risk losing your funds permanently.

import.title: Import wallet
import.help: You can import previously exported wallets. Wallet password stays within the exported file, so you can access it after the import with the same password as before. You can change the password after login from the settings page.

pay.title: Make payment - from
pay.note_is_b64: Note is Base64 encoded. If enabled you can post base64 input in the note field and send it as raw data to the network.
pay.signature_from_friend: Please enter signature from your friend here
pay.store_other_help: It is better to store the account to which you are sending money in the address book
pay.name: Name
pay.send_to_other_signators: Send this data to other signators
pay.state_sent: Payment sent to the network. Tx:
pay.state_confirmed: Confirmation has been received. Your payment is in the block
pay.state_error_not_sent: Payment has probably not reached the network. Are you offline? Please check you account
pay.pay_to_address_malformed: Pay To address seems to be malformed.
pay.asset_failed_to_load: It seems that desired asset has failed to load
pay.asset_too_small_balance: It seems your balance is below desired pay amount
pay.rekey_warning: Please pay special attention to this transaction. From now on, the account will use a different private key to be able to complete transactions. If you do not have the private key of the new account, you will most likely lose the ability to sign transactions.
pay.close_to_warning: Warning: This transaction will close the sender account and transfer its ENTIRE remaining balance to this address.
pay.asset_close_to_warning: Warning: This transaction will opt the sender out of the asset and transfer its ENTIRE asset balance to this address.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.q1: What private information about me do you collect?
privacy.a1: None. Besides IP address and browser which is stored in logs for every internet service, we do not collect any form of private information such as your name, email or address.
privacy.q2: What do you store in cookies?
privacy.a2: Nothing. We do not use cookies. We use persistent local storage called IndexedDB for the storage of your wallet.
privacy.q3: How do you collect analytics information?
privacy.a3: We do not. We do not use any form of analytics service, e.g. google analytics, so that you can feel safer. The website is purely HTML with javascript. We do know your IP address only when you make requests to the server. Depending on your settings and your algod server or indexer, the selected server also may know your IP address.
privacy.q4: Does use of this service affect the ads I see?
privacy.a4: No. Ad servers do not have any information from us about any usage. The only way how, e.g. google, may know you are using this service is if you access this site from google search.
privacy.a5: Scholtz & Company, jsa has created this service. Company Id: 51 882 272, VAT ID: SK2120828105, based in Bratislava, Slovakia.  If this is running in the official website a-wallet.net, the company is also the service provider. The company is not liable for any service, nor any errors in the application.

assetcreate.clawback: Clawback address: Specified address can revoke user asset holdings and send them to other addresses

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about {name}, Algorand and the Biatec ecosystem - from your first account to swaps, security and DApp connections.
faq.no_results_text: Try a different search term, or clear the search box to browse all categories.
faq.footer_note: {name} is free and open source. Read the code, report an issue or contribute a translation on

general.title: General Questions
general.q1: What is {name} and what is it for?
general.a1: {name} is a free, open-source, non-custodial wallet for the Algorand blockchain that runs entirely in your web browser. It lets you create and manage Algorand accounts, send and receive ALGO and Algorand Standard Assets (ASAs), sign transactions for decentralized applications, and interact with the Algorand ecosystem without ever handing your private keys to a third party.
general.q2: Do I need to register or provide personal information to use {name}?
general.a2: No. {name} has no backend server, no sign-up form, and no KYC. Everything is generated and stored locally in your browser, so you can start creating accounts immediately without giving up your email, phone number, or identity documents.
general.a3: Yes, {name} is completely free and open source. You only ever pay the standard Algorand network transaction fees (typically 0.001 ALGO), never a fee to the wallet itself.
general.q4: Which devices and browsers does {name} work on?
general.a4: {name} is a responsive web application that works on any modern desktop or mobile browser (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari) without installation. Because it is a single-page app with no native binary, the same wallet works identically on Windows, macOS, Linux, Android and iOS.
general.a5: {name} was originally created by Scholtz & Company, jsa. and is now developed under the {link1} brand. The project is open source, and anyone can review, audit or contribute to the code on {link2}.

biatec.title: Biatec Wallet & Ecosystem
biatec.a1: {link1} is the company and brand behind {name}, building a suite of open tools around the Algorand blockchain, including this wallet, a swap/exchange routing service, and a scheduled-payments service, all designed to make using Algorand simpler and safer.
biatec.q2: How does Biatec Wallet relate to Biatec's other services?
biatec.a2: Biatec Wallet is the entry point: it holds your keys and signs transactions locally, while other {link1} (such as the swap router used on the Swap page, or the scheduled-payments API used for recurring transfers) only ever receive unsigned transaction data or public information - never your private keys.
biatec.q3: Does Biatec run its own Algorand infrastructure?
biatec.a3: Yes. {link1} operates its own Algorand node, indexer and participation infrastructure that {name} can connect to, in addition to public Algorand nodes, giving you a choice of network providers directly in the wallet's settings.
biatec.q4: What is the Biatec swap router used for?
biatec.a4: When you use the Swap feature in {name}, your request is routed through {link1} aggregator, which compares prices across multiple Algorand decentralized exchanges (DEXs) to find you the best available rate, then returns an unsigned transaction for you to review and sign yourself.
biatec.q5: Is Biatec funded by Algorand Inc. or the Algorand Foundation?
biatec.a5: Biatec is an independent, community-driven project - it is not owned by, and does not receive ongoing funding from, Algorand Inc. or the Algorand Foundation. It has, however, received several one-off grants through the Algorand Foundation's {link1}, including grants for {name} itself, for {link2}, and for Biatec DEX. The Algorand name and logo are used under Algorand's official media guidelines, purely to indicate blockchain compatibility.

blockchain.title: Algorand & Blockchain Basics
blockchain.a1: A blockchain is a distributed, tamper-resistant public ledger maintained by many independent computers (nodes) instead of a single company or bank. Every transaction is grouped into a block, cryptographically linked to the previous block, and replicated across the network, so no single party can alter history unnoticed.
blockchain.q2: What is Algorand and why does {name} use it?
blockchain.a2: Algorand is a fast, low-fee, carbon-negative public blockchain that uses a Pure Proof-of-Stake consensus, meaning transactions finalize in a few seconds without energy-intensive mining. {name} builds on Algorand because it offers instant finality, predictable sub-cent fees, and native support for tokens and smart contracts.
blockchain.q3: What is a public/private key pair?
blockchain.a3: Every Algorand account is a pair of cryptographic keys: a public key (your address, which is safe to share) and a private key (your secret, which must never be shared). The private key mathematically proves ownership by signing transactions; anyone who has it can spend your funds, so protecting it is the single most important part of using a wallet.
blockchain.q4: What is a wallet, and does it "store" my coins?
blockchain.a4: A wallet does not actually hold your coins - your ALGO and assets always live on the Algorand blockchain itself. A wallet like {name} stores and protects your private keys and gives you an interface to view your balances and sign transactions that move funds recorded on-chain.
blockchain.q5: What are transaction fees on Algorand, and why are they so low?
blockchain.a5: Algorand charges a minimal, fixed transaction fee (currently 0.001 ALGO, a fraction of a cent) regardless of network congestion, unlike blockchains with auction-based fees that spike under load. This predictability is one of the reasons Algorand is popular for payments and everyday transactions.

security.title: Security & Privacy
security.q1: How is my wallet data secured?
security.a1: Your data never leaves your browser. Every wallet is encrypted with AES-256 using your password before being saved to your browser's local IndexedDB storage. As long as you choose a strong password, brute-forcing the encryption is computationally infeasible.
security.q2: Does my wallet password ever get sent over the internet?
security.a2: Never. Your password is used only locally to encrypt and decrypt your wallet in the browser and is never transmitted to any server, including Biatec's own infrastructure.
security.q3: What happens if I close the browser tab or my computer sleeps?
security.a3: {name} automatically locks itself and clears the decrypted password from memory 5 minutes after your last activity, so a device left unattended does not leave your wallet exposed. You will need to re-enter your password to unlock it again.
security.q4: What should I do if I suspect my device is compromised?
security.a4: Immediately rekey or move funds from any account whose private key may have been exposed to a fresh account created on a clean device, since a compromised private key can never be made secure again - only replaced. Also change your wallet password and review connected WalletConnect sessions and revoke any you do not recognize.
security.a5: Yes, security audit reports are published openly in the project's GitHub repository so anyone can review what has been checked and what was found, reflecting the project's open-source, transparency-first approach.

accounts.title: Account Types & Setup
accounts.q1: What types of accounts can I create in {name}?
accounts.a1: {name} supports plain Ed25519 accounts (a standard private key), ARC-76 accounts derived deterministically from an email and password, HD (ARC-52 / BIP32-Ed25519) wallets that can generate unlimited accounts from one recovery phrase, multisignature accounts requiring multiple approvals, and Ledger hardware wallet accounts, plus support for viewing rekeyed accounts.
accounts.q2: What is an HD wallet and why would I use one?
accounts.a2: An HD (hierarchical deterministic) wallet stores a single 24-word recovery phrase and can mathematically derive an unlimited number of separate Algorand accounts from it, so you only ever need to back up one phrase instead of one per account. This is the recommended account type if you plan to manage many accounts.
accounts.q3: What is a multisignature (multisig) account and when should I use one?
accounts.a3: A multisignature account requires a minimum number of signatures from a chosen group of accounts before a transaction is valid, for example 2-of-3 co-signers. It is useful for shared treasuries, organizational funds, or any situation where you want to remove single points of failure from spending decisions.
accounts.q4: Can I use a Ledger hardware wallet with {name}?
accounts.a4: Yes. {name} supports Ledger hardware devices, which keep your private key isolated on the physical device itself and require you to physically confirm each transaction on the device screen, giving you the highest level of protection against malware on your computer.
accounts.q5: How do I create a new account or import an existing one?
accounts.a5: From the accounts list, choose "New account" to generate a fresh Ed25519, HD, ARC-76, multisig or Ledger account, or choose "Import" to recover an account from an existing 25-word mnemonic, HD recovery phrase, or backup file exported from another device.

actions.title: Account Actions
actions.q1: How do I send and receive ALGO or assets?
actions.a1: Open an account and use the "Pay" action to send ALGO or any opted-in asset to another address, or share your account's address or QR code from the "Receive" action so someone else can send funds to you.
actions.q2: What does "opt-in" mean and why do I need to do it?
actions.a2: Algorand requires every account to explicitly opt in to an Algorand Standard Asset (ASA) before it can hold or receive that token, which reserves a small amount of ALGO as minimum balance and prevents accounts from being spammed with unwanted tokens. You can opt in to an asset from the Assets tab of your account.
actions.q3: What is rekeying and what is it used for?
actions.a3: Rekeying lets you change which private key controls an account without changing the account's address, for example to move authority from a plain key to a multisig group, or to a Ledger device, while keeping all your assets, apps and transaction history exactly where they are. It is a powerful, irreversible action, so always double-check the new authorized address before signing.
actions.q4: How do I schedule a future or recurring payment?
actions.a4: Use the Scheduled Payments feature on an account page to define a transaction template and a future date or recurring interval; {name}, via {link1} scheduler service, will prepare the unsigned transaction and prompt you to sign and broadcast it when it becomes due.
actions.q5: How do I interact with a smart contract or DApp from {name}?
actions.a5: Connect {name} to the DApp using WalletConnect, either by scanning a QR code or pasting a connect URI, and every transaction the DApp requests will appear in {name} for you to review in full detail and explicitly sign before anything is broadcast.

assets.title: Assets & Tokens
assets.q1: What is an Algorand Standard Asset (ASA)?
assets.a1: An ASA is any token natively issued on the Algorand blockchain, using the same built-in mechanism for both fungible tokens (like stablecoins or reward points) and non-fungible tokens (NFTs), without needing a separate smart contract per token as on some other chains.
assets.q2: How do NFTs work on Algorand?
assets.a2: An Algorand NFT is simply an ASA with a total supply of 1 and 0 decimal places, often carrying metadata (image, attributes) referenced via an IPFS or Arweave URL stored in the asset's configuration. {name} displays these asset media previews directly in your Assets tab.
assets.q3: Why do I see a small ALGO balance reserved that I can't spend?
assets.a3: Algorand requires every account to keep a minimum balance in ALGO, which increases with each asset you opt into and each app you interact with, to prevent ledger bloat from empty or spam accounts. This reserved amount is returned to you if you close out of the assets or apps.
assets.q4: How can I tell if a token is legitimate before opting in?
assets.a4: Anyone can create an ASA with any name or ticker, including fake tokens impersonating well-known projects, so always verify the exact Asset ID from the issuing project's official website or documentation rather than trusting the name alone, and check the asset's creator address and details in {name} or a block explorer before opting in.
assets.q5: Can I hide unwanted or spam assets from my account view?
assets.a5: Yes, {name} lets you mark specific assets as hidden or filter low-value/unverified assets from the main Assets tab, so unsolicited airdropped tokens do not clutter your view while remaining fully in your control if you need them later.

swap.title: Swapping & DeFi
swap.a1: The Swap page lets you exchange one Algorand asset for another directly from your wallet. {name} queries {link1} router to find the best price across multiple on-chain decentralized exchanges, then builds an unsigned transaction group that you review and sign yourself - funds never pass through a custodial intermediary.
swap.q2: What is slippage and why does the swap page ask me to set it?
swap.a2: Slippage tolerance is the maximum price movement you accept between the moment you request a quote and the moment your transaction actually settles on-chain; setting it too low can cause your swap to fail in a fast-moving market, while setting it too high can result in a worse-than-expected price.
swap.q3: What are liquidity pools and why do they determine my swap price?
swap.a3: A liquidity pool is a smart contract holding reserves of two assets that traders swap against; the price you get is set algorithmically by the ratio of those reserves at the moment of your trade, and larger trades relative to pool size move the price more (higher price impact).
swap.q4: Are there fees when I swap?
swap.a4: A swap involves the standard Algorand network fee for each transaction in the group, plus a small liquidity-provider fee set by the specific DEX pool being used, both of which are shown to you in the swap preview before you confirm and sign.
swap.a5: No. At every step your assets remain under your own private key's control; {name} only ever presents an unsigned transaction for the swap, which you must explicitly review and sign, exactly like any other transaction in the wallet.

connect.title: WalletConnect & DApps
connect.q1: What is WalletConnect and why would I use it?
connect.a1: WalletConnect is an open protocol that lets {name} securely connect to third-party decentralized applications (DApps) - such as marketplaces, DEXs or games - running in a separate browser tab or device, so the DApp can request signatures without ever seeing your private key.
connect.q2: How do I connect {name} to a DApp?
connect.a2: Open the Connect page in {name}, then either scan the QR code shown by the DApp with your camera or paste the "wc:" connection URI it provides, and approve the session request that appears, choosing which of your accounts to expose to that DApp.
connect.q3: Is it safe to approve a transaction request from a DApp?
connect.a3: Only if you understand what it does. {name} decodes and displays every field of the requested transaction (type, sender, receiver, amount, asset, fee, rekey target and more) before you sign, so always read the full detail rather than clicking Sign reflexively, especially for app calls and rekey requests.
connect.q4: How do I disconnect a DApp session?
connect.a4: Open the Connect page, find the active session under "Sessions", and select Disconnect; this immediately revokes the DApp's ability to send you further signing requests, though it can still ask you to reconnect at any time.
connect.q5: What should I check to verify a DApp is genuine before connecting?
connect.a5: {name} highlights domain verification and genesis network match warnings on incoming connection and transaction requests; pay close attention to any "domain mismatch" or "unverified" warning, and only connect to DApp URLs you obtained from the project's official website or documentation.

backup.title: Backup, Recovery & Support
backup.q1: How do I back up my wallet?
backup.a1: Open Settings, go to the Backup section, and export an encrypted backup file of your wallet to a location of your choice, such as a USB drive or password manager attachment; for individual accounts, you can also record the 25-word mnemonic or HD recovery phrase directly, which works independently of {name}.
backup.q2: How can I move my wallet to another computer or browser?
backup.a2: Export a backup file from Settings on the original device, then on the new device choose "Import wallet" and upload that file; you will unlock it with the same password as before, which you can then change from Settings if you wish.
backup.q3: What happens if I forget my wallet password?
backup.a3: The password only unlocks the encrypted copy stored in that specific browser - it does not exist anywhere else, so if you have no backup and forget it, the funds are unrecoverable by design, which is what makes the wallet secure. This is why keeping an independent backup of your account mnemonics is essential.
backup.q4: How can I view or contribute to the source code?
backup.a4: {name} is fully open source at {link1}; anyone may read, audit, fork or submit a pull request, and translation contributions are especially welcome via the src/locales folder.
backup.q5: How can I get help or support the project?
backup.a5: You can join the project's {link1} for community support, open an issue on {link2} for bugs or feature requests, contribute code or translations, or support ongoing development with a donation to the project's Algorand address listed in the app footer. {name} is not funded by Algorand Inc. or the Algorand Foundation, though it has received Algorand Foundation xGov community grants for specific features.

new_account_2fa.title: 2FA Account
new_account_2fa.help1: 2FA account allows you to create multisig account with 3 accounts setup with threshold of 2 required for signature.
new_account_2fa.help2: First account may be password protected account where in the case of the hack of your wallet the hacker cannot sign the transactions on your behalf. If your 2FA device gets broken, or 2FA service gets down, you can rekey your account to another 2FA multisig with your first and second account.
new_account_2fa.help3: 2FA service is third party service which might be compromised. If hacker receives your private key and receives the private key of the 2fa service, he may sign transactions on your behalf. By using the 2fa service you increase chance that hacker who receive ability to sign transactions with your primary or recovery account is not able to sign transactions from your multisig account.
new_account_2fa.scan_qr: Please scan the QR code with your 2FA app
new_account_2fa.confirm_help: Please write 2FA code from your auth app to confirm the setup. After confirmation it is not possible to request the 2fa secret code again.

onlineofflinedialog.header: Protect the network with your account's balance
onlineofflinedialog.warning: You can select public participation node in order to verify the blocks in the network. You will generate new participation keys at the public algod server and sign the transaction to allow these participation keys collect blocks data. This will cost you as one transaction on the network (0.001 algo). Online account state does not mean that your private key is exposed to the internet. It means that your account is registered at the online algod node which is protecting blockchain.
onlineofflinedialog.stakingHelp: By paying 2 algorand now as network fee, and having more than 30k algos on your account, you are eligible to receive the staking rewards anytime the staking node mints new block.
onlineofflinedialog.onlineRounds: Number of rounds to protect the network

new_account_shamir.help: Shamir backup is the method to split mnemonic into N parts where M accounts is the recovery threshold.

account_export.help: On this page you can export your private key in form of algorand's mnemonic or shamir backup.
account_export.shamir_help: Please secure mnemonic at very secure place. Make sure you write down all parts of shamir backup and do not store the recovery threshold at one place.
account_export.algo_help: Please secure mnemonic at very secure place.

scheduled_payments.title: Scheduled payments management
scheduled_payments.description: With decentralized scheduler you will deploy the smart contract - escrow account which will be used for purpose to allow any executor perform withdrawal from your escrow account to the destination account when the time comes. Executors are incentivized to execute your scheduled task with vision of receiving small fee paid in ASA.Gold token - 0.001 gram of gold.
scheduled_payments.no_payment_found: No scheduled payments has been found
scheduled_payments.description_detail: In this detail overview of the scheduled payment you can see the current asset balance of the escrow account and you have actions to deposit more assets to the escrow account or to executors.
